title: (Sacramento) Assemblymember David Alvarez (D-San Diego) announced that the Port of San Diego Reform and Accountability Act, Assembly Bill 2783, passed off the Assembly Floor unanim

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Port of San Diego has very little statutory oversight despite being reviewed by multiple grand jury reports over the past 35 years.",
    "AB 2783 adopts recommendations identified in the 2022-2023 Grand Jury report to improve transparency and oversight within the Port.",
    "Communities adjacent to the Port of San Diego experience some of the worst air quality in the state, scoring in the 100th and 99th percentile for exposure to diesel particulate matter.",
    "According to CalEPA’s CalEnviroScreen 4.0 tool, census tracts near the Port score in the 95th percentile for asthma and poverty rates.",
    "AB 2783 would increase the Port’s set-aside for projects aimed at mitigating health impacts for residents living near maritime industrial activity.",
    "The legislation creates the Maritime Industrial Impact Fund to enhance public access and mitigate impacts in underinvested communities."
  ]
}
```
